answer.can_i_mine_hobc_with_asics: Yes, use SHA-256 ASICs on the Main Pool.
answer.can_i_use_a_btc_address: No. Use a real HOBC address only.
answer.can_small_miners_try: Yes, use the Nano Pool at difficulty 0.005.
answer.does_the_pool_split_rewards: No. HOBC pools are solo only.
answer.is_mining_guaranteed_income: No. Payouts happen only when your worker finds a block.
body.p1: Use the Main Pool for normal SHA-256 ASIC miners and higher-hashrate devices. Start difficulty is 5000.
body.p10: Create a HOBC address with the Windows standalone wallet, another local wallet you control, or the custodial web wallet for small balances. The web wallet is custodial and is better treated as a convenience wallet.
body.p11: Check the pool URL, port, worker format, password, SHA-256 support, and whether you accidentally used a BTC, BCH, or DGB address.
body.p12: Use the explorer and stats pages to check real chain height, latest blocks, pool status, reserve, and burn information.
body.p2: Use this when your miner is a regular ASIC or has enough hashrate that very low difficulty is not needed.
body.p3: Use the Nano Pool for nano miners, test miners, and very low-hashrate SHA-256 devices. Start difficulty is 0.005.
body.p4: Use this when your miner needs a very low share difficulty to show accepted shares.
body.p5: The HOBC Nano Pool is the best starting point for low-hashrate SHA-256 miners that need very low share difficulty. This includes nano miner, Bitaxe-style, NerdMiner-style, USB test miner, and other small hobby mining setups that may not submit shares often on normal pool difficulty.
body.p6: Use the Nano Pool URL, a real HOBC address, and a simple worker suffix. Accepted shares mean your miner is connected and submitting valid work, but HOBC pools are solo only, so payouts happen only when a worker finds a real block.
body.p7: Solo mining means one worker wins the block. The pool tracks shares so you can see that your miner is connected and working, but shares are not split payouts.
body.p8: If your worker finds a real HOBC block, the payout goes to the HOBC address before the dot in your worker name. If no block is found, there is no payout yet.

heading.nano_miner_and_small_miner_setup: Nano Miner and Small Miner Setup
hero.lead: HOBC mining is SHA-256 solo mining for home miners, nano miners, small test miners, and regular ASICs. This page gives beginners a starting point: get a HOBC address, choose the right pool, enter the miner settings, and understand when payouts happen.
hero.title: Start Mining HobbyHash Coin
label.can_i_mine_hobc_with_asics: Can I mine HOBC with ASICs?
label.can_i_use_a_btc_address: Can I use a BTC address?
meta.description: Beginner-friendly HobbyHash Coin solo mining setup for nano miners, small SHA-256 miners, Bitaxe-style devices, NerdMiner-style hobby miners, and ASICs.
meta.title: Mine HOBC With Nano and Small SHA-256 Miners
notice.1: Important: HOBC pools are solo only. Accepted shares show your miner is working, but a payout happens only if your worker finds a real HOBC block. The pool does not split every block reward across all miners.
value.1_get_an_address: Use the Windows standalone wallet, another local HOBC wallet, or the custodial web wallet for small balances.
value.2_pick_a_pool: Main Pool for ASICs, Nano Pool for tiny miners.
value.5_watch_status: Use pool pages and stats for real data only.
